title: languas oxyphylla
intro: Languas oxyphylla, also known as the lesser galangal or Thai ginger, is a type of plant that belongs to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae) and is native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Languas oxyphylla include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and vomiting, and reduce fever, as well as its potential to help manage respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Languas oxyphylla can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in cooking and as a flavoring agent in traditional Thai and Southeast Asian cuisine.
